summary: The test suite covers all four core tagging conventions (latest, pr-{n}, semver, branch-sha), sanitization rules, short-SHA extraction, and basic error handling, mapping cleanly to the task requirements. Design is excellent: Describe/Context/It hierarchy is logical, test names are precise, TDD phase comments aid traceability, and tests are fully independent with no shared mutable state. Rigor is good but has a few gaps: (1) git tags on non-main branches are untested — the implementation silently emits both the tag and the branch-sha tag in that case; (2) PR combined with git tags is untested (implementation drops the tags, which is a debatable but consequential behavior); (3) whitespace-only inputs are not checked (only empty string); (4) there is no assertion that a PR context returns exactly one tag. These gaps mean a regression in combination-mode behavior could slip through, keeping overall quality high but short of exemplary.
